title: Bench with Shelf and Scrap-Wood Top
summary: A small entry bench with 1x12 end panels and a mitered apron, topped with nine short 1x4 boards nailed on side by side — a genuine use for shorter offcuts instead of one wide board.
description:
The two end panels are each built from a pair of 1x12s — a shelf-height piece and a taller back piece — joined together, with a mitered 1x4 apron running between the ends at 45° on both long edges to tie the assembly together and support the top.

**The top is nine short 1x4 boards laid side by side rather than a single wide board**, which is the entire point of this bench — it's built to use up shorter offcuts from other projects instead of buying a full board for the top. Nail each one on with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, keeping consistent gaps between boards for a clean plank look.

Build it with pocket holes throughout, or with 2" self-tapping star bit screws if a pocket-hole jig isn't on hand — both joinery methods are shown as valid in the original design, so pick whichever matches your existing tools.

tools.note: Or use self-tapping star bit screws instead.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping star bit screws, 2"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 45° off square, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Build the end panels
steps.body: Join the lower and upper 1x12 pieces to build two matching end panels.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping star bit screws, 2"

steps.title: Attach the apron
steps.body: Miter the apron ends at 45° and attach between the two end panels along both long edges.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping star bit screws, 2"

steps.title: Attach the top boards
steps.body: Nail the nine top boards on side by side with consistent gaps, using 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small bench with plank-style scrap wood top
